hypotheses.statement: PMs will pay for AI tooling that reduces manual PM work by >10hrs/week because the time saved is worth 10x the cost of the tool
hypotheses.validation_method: Willingness-to-pay interviews with 10 PMs showing >80% would pay >= $49/seat/month for 10hrs/week savings
hypotheses.kill_criteria: If <50% of interviewed PMs are willing to pay >= $39/seat/month, value hypothesis is invalidated

hypotheses.statement: Enterprise PMs value evidence traceability (source citations on AI outputs) more than output depth — they will accept shallower AI output if every claim is sourced
hypotheses.validation_method: A/B test: PMs shown same output with and without source citations. Preference measured.
hypotheses.kill_criteria: If <60% of PMs prefer sourced output over deeper unsourced output, traceability is over-weighted in product design

hypotheses.statement: Prototype generation (3 interactive HTML variants) significantly reduces PM-to-engineering handoff ambiguity compared to text-only PRDs — sprint carry-over drops from 30% to <15%
hypotheses.validation_method: Measure sprint carry-over rate before and after prototype handoff on 3 engineering teams over 3 sprints
hypotheses.kill_criteria: If sprint carry-over does not drop below 20% after 3 sprints of prototype handoffs, prototype investment is not justified by ambiguity reduction

hypotheses.statement: PESTLE analysis is a 10x differentiator — no competitor offers it and PMs report it as a top-3 capability they need but cannot get from existing tools
hypotheses.validation_method: Competitive feature matrix confirms absence across all monitored competitors. PM interviews confirm PESTLE as top-3 unmet need.
hypotheses.kill_criteria: If competitor ships PESTLE analysis OR <40% of PMs rank PESTLE in top-5 needs, differentiator claim weakens

hypotheses.statement: Product Health Dashboard with top-5 recommended actions increases weekly PM proactive decision rate by 40% vs dashboard-only without recommendations
hypotheses.validation_method: A/B test: Group A sees dashboard with recommended actions. Group B sees dashboard without. Measure: decisions made per week, time to action
hypotheses.kill_criteria: If decision rate increase is <20%, recommended actions feature is not delivering expected PM leverage

interdependencies.description: If value hypothesis is invalidated, enterprise traceability hypothesis becomes irrelevant — no one will pay for the tool at all
Traceability on prototypes (not just text PRDs) amplifies engineering handoff quality — both hypotheses reinforce each other
If PESTLE is not a differentiator, the strategic value hypothesis weakens — differentiation is part of why PMs would pay

risk_prioritization.risk_heat_assessment: Highest risk is prototype effectiveness (h_tact_02) — high uncertainty with transformative impact. Strategic value hypothesis (h_strat_01) is next — medium uncertainty with transformative impact. PESTLE differentiator (h_tact_03) is lowest risk — already partially validated by competitive matrix.

portfolio_summary.risk_distribution: Highest risk: h_tact_02 (92) — prototype effectiveness. Second highest: h_strat_01 (85) — value hypothesis. Low risk: h_tact_03 (45) — PESTLE differentiator.

prototype_routing.rationale: Highest risk score (92). Prototype effectiveness is critical to ProductOS value and is highly uncertain. Generate 3 interactive HTML prototypes and test with engineering teams before committing to full prototype pipeline.
Dashboard recommended actions feature has high PM leverage potential but medium uncertainty. Prototype a dashboard mockup with realistic action recommendations for PM testing.
